old_claim.note: Protein Engineering is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: Zinc-finger nucleases require engineered protein-domain architecture: zinc-finger DNA-binding domains are fused to FokI cleavage domains to create sequence-directed nucleases.

source_shape.source_locator: Title, abstract, and experimental framing describing zinc-finger proteins fused to the FokI cleavage domain as hybrid restriction enzymes.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper reports zinc-finger DNA-binding proteins fused to the FokI cleavage domain to make hybrid enzymes with sequence-directed DNA cleavage behavior.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This licenses a hard architecture edge because the scoped ZFN technology is defined by engineered DNA-binding protein domains joined to a nuclease domain, rather than by protein engineering as background field context.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: zinc_finger_nucleases remain modeled as a protein-engineered nuclease platform, now with primary-source evidence and a corrected first-known date.
would_reject_if: A source showed zinc-finger nucleases can exist without zinc-finger DNA-binding protein domains fused or otherwise engineered with nuclease cleavage domains.
The ZFN node were broadened to all genome-editing nucleases rather than zinc-finger nuclease systems.
A better primary source showed the 1996 fusion-enzyme paper was only a precursor and did not establish the architecture modeled by this node.
short_reason: ZFNs are engineered zinc-finger/FokI protein-domain nucleases, so protein engineering is a required architecture dependency.
